macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Major Indices Close Lower on March 19 with Modest Declines
items.summary: The S&P 500 fell 0.27% to 6606.49, Nasdaq 100 down 0.29% to 24355.275, and Dow Jones down 0.44% to around 46,021, amid lighter trading volume and a VIX spike indicating heightened anxiety. Russell 2000 outperformed, rising 0.65%, while energy sector gained 1.59%.

items.title: Silver Prices Plunge Nearly 10% on March 19 Amid Dollar Strength and Fed Uncertainty
items.summary: Spot silver dropped nearly 10% intraday from near $80 to around $73.21 per ounce, driven by a stronger US dollar, rising bond yields, and uncertainty following the March 17-18 FOMC meeting. The selloff erased recent gains and pressured precious metals.

items.title: Global Central Banks Maintain Hawkish Stance as US Markets Dip
items.summary: Central banks worldwide held firm on hawkish policies amid US stock declines, with all major indices closing below their 200-day moving averages except outperforming small-caps. Tesla led Mag 7 declines, falling over 3%, while gold stocks weakened.

items.title: NEXT plc Announces Share Buyback Transaction
items.summary: UK retailer NEXT plc executed a transaction in own shares on March 19 under its irrevocable buyback programme.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Critical Bounce Amid Quadruple Witching Expiry and Kalshi Ban Update
items.summary: Bitcoin is showing signs of a critical bounce setup as trillions in derivatives expire during quadruple witching, potentially causing high volatility across crypto and traditional markets. A Nevada appeals court has issued a temporary ban on Kalshi prediction markets, impacting retail traders and future regulations.

items.title: US SEC Clarifies Most Major Cryptos Are Not Securities, Boosting Bitcoin and Others
items.summary: The US SEC has issued guidelines stating that most major cryptocurrencies, including Bitcoin, Ethereum, and XRP, are not securities but digital commodities, reducing regulatory uncertainty and increasing institutional adoption prospects.

items.title: Bitcoin Breaks Key Support Level, Faces Potential Drop to New Lows
items.summary: Bitcoin has lost a critical support level, with analysts warning of a possible larger drop below current lows or a push toward $80,000 depending on momentum indicators like VWAP and red dots.

items.title: Crypto Market Structure Evolving with AI-Driven Liquidity and Institutional Flows
items.summary: Experts discuss how crypto cycles are shifting beyond four-year patterns, driven by ETF inflows, AI agents, and macro liquidity, positioning the market for exponential adoption.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Pentagon Launches Agent Builder for Rapid AI Agent Creation
items.summary: The Pentagon's Chief Digital and Artificial Intelligence Office introduced Agent Builder in early March 2026, enabling employees to create customized AI agents; within two weeks, 20,000 agents were built to support daily workflows across military components.[5]

items.title: Agentic AI Hailed as Next Leap for Clinical Trial Data Management
items.summary: Agentic AI, using autonomous agents for tasks, learning, and decision-making, is positioned to transform 8 out of 10 workflows in clinical trials and impact up to 95% of life science roles, addressing data volume and oversight challenges.[3]

items.title: Mistral Unveils Forge Platform for Custom AI Models
items.summary: On March 18, 2026, Mistral launched Forge, a platform allowing enterprises and governments to build custom AI models trained from scratch on their data.[1]

items.title: QCraft CEO Predicts Physical AI as Future Direction
items.summary: QCraft CEO Dr. James Yu shared his vision for physical AI at Munich's Intelligent Vehicles & Production Conference on March 20, 2026, emphasizing hardware-integrated AI advancements.[7]

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Crimson Desert Launches to Mixed Reviews and Stock Plunge
items.summary: Crimson Desert, the open-world action-adventure game from Pearl Abyss, released on March 19, 2026, for PS5, Xbox Series X/S, PC, and macOS, earning a Metacritic score of 78—lower than expected—and causing a 29-30% drop in the developer's stock price despite strong pre-order sales.[1][2]

items.title: Borderlands 3 Black Market Vending Machine Location Updated
items.summary: The location of Maurice's Black Market vending machine in Borderlands 3 has been revealed for March 19-26, 2026, with players noting a last-minute change near the hinted spot.[3]

items.title: Streamers Dive into Crimson Desert on Launch Day
items.summary: Content creators like York Street Gaming and Maximilian Dood are streaming Crimson Desert following its March 19 release, highlighting excitement amid review controversies.[4][6]

items.title: Crimson Desert Review Debate: Masterpiece or Mid?
items.summary: Critics are split on Crimson Desert's Metacritic score of 78, with discussions on gameplay mechanics, console performance issues, and its impact on Pearl Abyss's market value.[2]

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.
